_description: Four realistic demo scenarios covering the key document categories: SWIFT wire transfer (AML/financial crime path), residential loan application (credit underwriting path), trade confirmation (trading surveillance path), and a regulatory exam letter (regulatory change path). Each scenario includes a simulated raw document text excerpt and the expected extraction output, allowing the Streamlit demo to run without needing to upload actual documents.

scenarios.scenario_name: SWIFT MT103 — High-Value Wire Transfer (AML Alert Path)
scenarios.scenario_description: A SWIFT MT103 customer credit transfer for $950,000 originating from a correspondent bank in a high-risk jurisdiction. The wire amount is below the CTR threshold but high enough to trigger AML monitoring. The ordering customer has an unusual name match pattern. This scenario demonstrates the financial crime routing path and shows how the agent flags AML risk indicators before routing to Agent 01.
scenarios.raw_document_text:
{1:F01FNBKUS33AXXX1234567890}{2:I103BKCHCH22AXXXN}{3:{108:MT103 20240315}}{4:
:20:REF20240315-0042
:23B:CRED
:32A:240315USD950000,00
:50K:/****4872
ALPHA TRADING INTERNATIONAL LTD
14 HARBOR BLVD, LIMASSOL
CYPRUS CY-3025

SAN FRANCISCO CA 94111

anomaly_flags: Ordering bank BIC BKCHCH22 (Credit Suisse successor) — Swiss correspondent with elevated AML monitoring
Ordering customer address: Cyprus — FATF-monitored jurisdiction for shell company risk
Remittance purpose 'CONSULTING FEES' is a common layering description — verify underlying contract
Amount $950,000 — near CTR threshold ($10,000 cash threshold inapplicable, but high-value wire monitoring applies)
Beneficiary is an LLC in San Francisco — verify beneficial ownership via FinCEN CDD records
expected_extraction.routing_rationale: SWIFT MT103 auto-routes to financial crime investigation and fraud detection per DOCUMENT_ROUTING table. High confidence extraction. Anomaly flags passed to downstream agents for investigation.

scenarios.scenario_name: Residential Loan Application — Geographic Fair Lending Flag
scenarios.scenario_description: A 1003 residential mortgage application from a borrower with a strong credit profile. The property address falls within a census tract that has historically shown disparate impact patterns. This demonstrates the fair lending screening path and the agent's ability to extract structured underwriting data from an unstructured PDF application form.
scenarios.raw_document_text:
UNIFORM RESIDENTIAL LOAN APPLICATION
Form 1003 (Rev. 11/2020)

BORROWER INFORMATION
Applicant Name: James R. Wilson
SSN: ***-**-6789 (masked)
Date of Birth: 1978-06-15
Phone: (415) 555-0192
Email: james.wilson@email.example.com

EMPLOYMENT
Employer: Pacific Tech Solutions Inc.
Position: Senior Software Engineer
Years Employed: 7
Base Salary: $185,000 per year

LOAN INFORMATION
Loan Amount: $875,000
Loan Purpose: Purchase
Loan Type: Conventional
Loan Term: 30 years
Subject Property: 1847 Ridgemont Ave, Oakland, CA 94602
Property Type: Single Family Residence
Estimated Value: $1,100,000

FINANCIAL INFORMATION
Monthly Debt Payments: $2,100 (auto loan $650, student loans $1,450)
Credit Score: 742
Down Payment: $225,000 (20.45%)
Down Payment Source: Savings

RACE / ETHNICITY (Government Monitoring - HMDA)
Race: Applicant declined to provide
Ethnicity: Applicant declined to provide
Sex: Male

Applicant Signature: James R. Wilson
Date: 2024-03-12

anomaly_flags: Property address 94602 census tract — flagged for geographic fair lending review. Verify lending patterns in this area do not show disparate impact under HMDA data.
Borrower declined race/ethnicity — HMDA required field. Record 'Information Not Provided' per HMDA code 7.
DTI: ($2,100/month debt + est. $4,520/month PITI) / ($185,000/12) = 6,620 / 15,417 = 42.9% — within Fannie Mae 45% limit.
expected_extraction.routing_rationale: High confidence extraction. Residential loan application auto-routes to credit underwriting. Fair lending flag passed as anomaly for underwriter review.

scenarios.scenario_name: Trade Confirmation — Equity Block Trade (Surveillance Path)
scenarios.scenario_description: A trade confirmation for a large block purchase of a technology stock executed shortly before the company's earnings announcement. The timing pattern warrants insider trading review by the trading surveillance agent. Demonstrates the securities trading document extraction and surveillance routing path.
scenarios.raw_document_text:
TRADE CONFIRMATION
First National Securities LLC
Member FINRA / SIPC

Date: March 11, 2024
Trade Date: 2024-03-11
Settlement Date: 2024-03-13 (T+2)

ACCOUNT: ****-****-2281
Account Name: Meridian Capital Partners LP

TRANSACTION DETAILS:
Action: BUY
Security: NEXON TECHNOLOGIES INC
Ticker: NXTN
CUSIP: 654321AB9
ISIN: US654321AB91
Quantity: 250,000 shares
Execution Price: $47.82 per share
Total Consideration: $11,955,000.00
Commission: $11,955.00
Net Amount Due: $11,966,955.00

EXECUTING BROKER: First National Securities LLC
TRADER ID: TDR-0042
STRATEGY: BLOCK
CLEARING: DTC

This confirmation is subject to the rules and regulations of FINRA and the DTCC.
Settlement instructions on file. Any discrepancies must be reported within 24 hours.

anomaly_flags: Block trade of 250,000 shares ($11.95M notional) — exceeds large trader threshold for FINRA Rule 4511 record retention.
Trade date 2024-03-11: Nexon Technologies (NXTN) reported earnings 2024-03-14 (3 days post-trade). Timing warrants insider trading review.
Trader TDR-0042: Confirm no access to material non-public information (MNPI) from investment banking deals involving NXTN during lookback window.
Counterparty Meridian Capital Partners LP: Verify not on restricted trading list as of trade date.
expected_extraction.routing_rationale: Trade confirmation auto-routes to trading surveillance. High confidence extraction. Timing anomaly flag passed as priority indicator.

scenarios.scenario_name: OCC Examination Letter — BSA/AML Matters Requiring Attention
scenarios.scenario_description: An OCC supervisory letter following a BSA/AML examination, identifying three Matters Requiring Attention (MRAs) related to transaction monitoring, customer risk rating, and SAR filing timeliness. This scenario demonstrates the regulatory change management routing path and the extraction of enforcement details that require a formal response.
scenarios.raw_document_text:
OFFICE OF THE COMPTROLLER OF THE CURRENCY
Washington, DC 20219

March 8, 2024

Board of Directors
First National Bank
123 Commerce Street
Chicago, IL 60601

Re: Report of Examination — Bank Secrecy Act / Anti-Money Laundering
Examination Period: January 2024
Rating: Needs Improvement

Dear Members of the Board:

This report summarizes the findings of the BSA/AML examination conducted during January 2024. The examination identified weaknesses in the bank's BSA/AML compliance program requiring corrective action.

MATTERS REQUIRING ATTENTION (3)

MRA 1 — Transaction Monitoring System Calibration
The bank's transaction monitoring system has not been calibrated since Q2 2022. Scenario thresholds are not risk-based and have produced an unacceptable false positive rate of 78%, impairing the quality of SAR investigations. The bank must engage a qualified independent consultant to assess and recalibrate the TMS by June 30, 2024.

MRA 2 — Customer Risk Rating Model
The customer risk rating model does not incorporate geographic risk or product risk factors as required under FinCEN's 2016 CDD Rule. Approximately 14,000 customer records must be re-risk-rated. A remediation plan is due within 60 days of this letter.

MRA 3 — SAR Filing Timeliness
The bank filed 23 SARs during the examination period with an average delay of 9 days beyond the 30-day filing deadline. Controls must be strengthened to ensure timely SAR filing consistent with 31 CFR 1020.320.

The bank is required to submit a written response addressing each MRA within 45 days of this letter. The Examiner-in-Charge, Sarah K. Thompson, may be contacted with questions.

Sincerely,
Office of the Comptroller of the Currency

extracted_fields.regulator_name: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
extracted_fields.findings_summary: Three MRAs: (1) TMS calibration, (2) customer risk rating model gaps, (3) SAR filing timeliness. Overall rating: Needs Improvement.
corrective_actions_required: Engage independent consultant to recalibrate TMS by 2024-06-30
Remediate 14,000 customer risk ratings within 60 days

anomaly_flags: 3 MRAs identified — triggers mandatory board notification and written response within 45 days (by 2024-04-22).
SAR filing delays (avg 9 days late) — potential civil money penalty exposure under 31 CFR 1010.820.
TMS false positive rate 78% — significantly above industry standard of 20-40%, indicating systemic calibration failure.
14,000 customer re-risk-ratings required — scope suggests remediation will require significant resources; begin triage immediately.
Examination rating 'Needs Improvement' — if repeated at next exam, may escalate to formal enforcement action.
expected_extraction.routing_rationale: Regulatory exam letter auto-routes to regulatory change management. High confidence extraction. MRA count and deadlines passed as priority fields.
